project: kcl/tregex — TRegEx-shaped regex API for bash over the [[ =~ ]] ERE engine (kklass static class)
status: COMPLETE (P0-P4) 2026-07-12, awaiting owner review, UNCOMMITTED (commits gated). Full TRegEx-shaped API over bash [[ =~ ]] ERE: isMatch/match/matches/split/replace/replaceCb/escape; RESULT-globals contract (static proc); i-flag; $-replacement grammar; maxCount on split+replace; FPC cross-check seed (008, over PCRE2, dialect-adjusted) + dialect report docs/ERE-vs-PCRE.md. Suite 001-009 = 190/190 on 5.2.37 AND true 5.3.9; final master sweep 15/15 (see P4 log). Deliverables: tregex.sh, tests/001-009+tests.sh, README.md, docs/TRegEx.md, docs/ERE-vs-PCRE.md, bench.sh, TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md, PLAN.md, ledger. Roadmap 2/7 -- next unit tarray. Phases: P0 (probes+static-proc contract+baseline), P1 (isMatch/match/escape 110), P2 (matches/split 144), P3 (replace/replaceCb + FPC integration 190), P4 (docs/bench).
notes: Single source of truth for tregex status. Plan: kcl/tregex/PLAN.md. API spec: Delphi System.RegularExpressions.TRegEx (DocWiki; .NET Regex behavior breaks ties). ENGINE: bash [[ =~ ]] POSIX ERE + BASH_REMATCH — NOT PCRE; every dialect delta is documented and pinned by tests, never silently emulated. FPC packages/regexpr (TRegExpr) is reference-only, its engine is not ported. Workflow: phase -> dual-bash -> master sweep -> STOP -> 'go'; commits gated.

baseline.measured: 2026-07-12 — at roadmap planning time (post-tdictionary). RE-MEASURE at P0: roadmap unit 1 (tstopwatch) will have raised the totals by the time tregex starts.

suites_green.kcl_breakdown: tcustomapplication 320, tdirectory 197, tfile 120, tlist 92, tpath 112, tstringhelper 393, tstringlist 155, dateutils 123, math 81, tdictionary 130

baseline.regression_rule: tregex work must keep the (re-measured) baseline green; the new tregex suite adds on top.

design_decisions.engine: bash [[ =~ ]] (POSIX ERE, C-speed, fork-free, internally cached compiled patterns). A bash-implemented engine would be ~100x slower and thousands of lines — rejected. The dialect delta vs PCRE is the documented price: no lazy quantifiers, no lookaround, no named groups, no pattern backrefs, POSIX classes instead of \d\w\s guarantees, leftmost-LONGEST alternation. Each delta gets a README table row AND a pinning test.
design_decisions.pattern_via_variable: Patterns are ALWAYS matched through a local variable (local __re="$2"; [[ $text =~ $__re ]]) — quoted RHS goes literal, inline (a|b) is a bash parse error (math P7 incident, pinned kcl idiom).
design_decisions.offsets: =~ gives no positions. Recovery: __pre=${text%%"${BASH_REMATCH[0]}"*}; RESULT_INDEX=${#__pre}. Exact for unanchored patterns (context-free substring matching); documented caveat for ^ $ \b anchored patterns (P0 probe matrix). Global scans iterate on the remainder with absolute-offset accounting.
design_decisions.flags: Single optional flags argument; v1 functional flag: 'i' (scoped nocasematch with fork-free save/restore on every return path). roMultiLine wontfix (no per-line anchors in =~); '.' matches newline by default (S1 probe).
design_decisions.global_scan: matches/replace/split loop on the remainder; EMPTY MATCH advances scan by one char (PCRE/.NET convention, pinned by test); maxCount optional bound on replace (bash extra).
design_decisions.replacement_grammar: $0..$9 groups, $$ literal dollar, unknown $x literal (DocWiki/.NET pin at P0). Pure-bash assembly from BASH_REMATCH — sed metachars (& \) are LITERAL in replacements (documented delta vs sed expectations).
design_decisions.errors: No match -> rc=1. INVALID PATTERN -> rc=2 (bash-native =~ signal, preserved and documented; debug msg under VERBOSE_KKLASS=debug).
design_decisions.static_class: No instances, no compiled-pattern objects: TRegEx.Create form is wontfix (bash caches compilation internally; an object adds dispatch cost and nothing else).

goals: TRegEx-shaped API (isMatch/match/matches/replace/split/escape + replaceCb extra) with RESULT/RESULT_INDEX/RESULT_LENGTH/RESULT_GROUPS contract and lossless nameref array fills.
Zero forks on every path incl. the i-flag save/restore; PATH='' tests.
The ERE-vs-PCRE delta table is part of the deliverable: every divergence documented AND pinned by a test (the delta is the spec).
Offset recovery exact for unanchored patterns, honestly-documented caveat for anchored ones (P0 probe matrix drives the wording).

test_basis.seed_primary: CORRECTED 2026-07-12: an FPC seed DOES exist (the earlier 'no FPC tests for this API' was wrong and unverified). FPC ships packages/vcl-compat/src/system.regularexpressions.pp = a Delphi-compatible TRegEx, with fpcunit tests packages/vcl-compat/tests/utcregexapi.pas (the TRegEx API) and utcregex.pas (the TPerlRegEx engine). CAVEAT: that engine is PCRE2 (unit says 'this unit uses PCRE2'), NOT POSIX ERE — so it is NOT a drop-in oracle; a large fraction of its cases exercise PCRE-only features this port wontfixes (named groups, lazy, lookaround, \d, \1-in-replacement). The DIALECT-COMPATIBLE subset IS adopted as a cross-check seed in tests/008_FpcParity.sh (fixture TestStr='xyz abba abbba abbbba zyx', TestExpr='a(b*)a'), with two adjustments: FPC Match.Index is 1-based -> mine 0-based (my=fpc-1); PCRE \s -> POSIX [[:space:]]. Basis for the rest: DocWiki TRegEx examples, .NET Regex behavior (Split/Replace/empty-match), POSIX ERE.

parity_model.tier_A_exact: Everything the ERE engine supports: byte-exact matches, group contents, offsets (unanchored), replacement assembly, split pieces, escape output, rc codes. Divergences from PCRE are pinned AS divergence tests.
parity_model.tier_FPC_crosscheck: 008_FpcParity.sh — the dialect-compatible subset of utcregexapi.pas/utcregex.pas, adapted for 0-based index and POSIX classes. Cases that do NOT port (named groups, \1-replacement, split-limit before it was added, per-group offsets, Escape wildcard mode) are catalogued as deltas in docs/ERE-vs-PCRE.md.

test_basis.new_coverage_protocol: Most tests are invented (DocWiki/.NET/POSIX/probe basis) and get a TEST_COVERAGE_NOTES.md row; the 008_FpcParity.sh cases are FPC-TRACEABLE (cite the utcregex*.pas procedure). The PCRE-vs-ERE deltas the FPC suite exposes are reported in docs/ERE-vs-PCRE.md.

out_of_scope.item: Lazy quantifiers, lookaround, named groups, pattern backrefs, inline modifiers (?i)(?x), roExplicitCapture/roIgnorePatternSpace/roCompiled/roNotEmpty
out_of_scope.reason: Not in POSIX ERE; silent emulation would lie about the engine. Documented in the README delta table.
bash =~ has no multiline mode; split lines first (kcl has the tools).
ERE is leftmost-longest; pinned by an explicit divergence test.
Replaced by RESULT_* + nameref fills (kcl house style); object protocol is pure dispatch overhead; bash caches compiled patterns internally.

execution_log.summary: tregex PLAN.md + ledger written; folders created. No implementation. Load-bearing decision recorded: TRegEx API shape over the bash ERE engine with a documented-and-tested delta table (never silent emulation); pattern-via-variable idiom mandatory; offset recovery via prefix-strip with anchored-pattern caveat to be probed at P0.

P0.1 + P0.2 executed. PROBE MATRIX S1-S12 run on GNU bash 5.2.37 AND 5.3.9 (secondary via PATH='/c/bin/msys64/usr/bin:$PATH' /c/bin/msys64/usr/bin/bash). HEADLINE: the two bashes are SEMANTICALLY IDENTICAL on every probe — same rc, matched text, offsets, group participation, word boundaries, alternation. The ONLY cross-bash difference: 5.3.9 prints a diagnostic to STDERR on an invalid/empty pattern (rc=2 on BOTH); 5.2.37 is silent. Load-bearing consequence: every user-pattern match uses `[[ $text =~ $__re ]] 2>/dev/null` — probed to (a) suppress the 5.3 stderr, (b) preserve rc=2, (c) stay FORK-FREE (redirect on the [[ ]] keyword does not spawn a subshell; verified by in-function var mutation surviving). Skeleton + runner GREEN 7/7 on both bashes.

probe_results.S1_dot_matches_newline: YES on both — '.' matches \n (subject is one string). DELTA vs PCRE/.NET default (where . excludes \n). Pin.
probe_results.S2_empty_pattern: rc=2 (INVALID) on both — bash rejects '' as 'empty (sub)expression'; NOT match-all. DECISION: preserve rc=2 (honest engine behavior, no emulation); documented delta vs .NET (which matches empty everywhere). Owner may override at P0 review.
probe_results.S3_invalid_pattern_rc: rc=2 on both for '[' '(' 'a{2,1}' '*' trailing-'\'. Distinct from rc=1 no-match. 5.3.9 leaks a stderr diagnostic -> suppressed via 2>/dev/null.
probe_results.S4_nocasematch: affects =~ AND == on both; default case-sensitive; `shopt -q nocasematch` is a FORK-FREE state query (rc reflects state). i-flag idiom confirmed.
probe_results.S5_offset_unanchored: EXACT on both. prefix-strip ${t%%"$m"*} gives 2 for ('xxabcabc','abc'), 0 for ('aaa','aa'), 0 for empty match ('zzz','a*').
probe_results.S6_offset_anchored_CAVEAT: CONFIRMED caveat: re='abc$' on 'abcXabc' matches the trailing 'abc' (TRUE offset 4) but prefix-strip finds the FIRST 'abc' -> reports 0. '^xx' reports 0 = TRUE 0 (start-anchored is safe). Documented: exact for unanchored; ^ $ \b \< \> with a recurring match-text can under-report. Scan CORRECTNESS unaffected (advance by string ops).
probe_results.S7_word_boundaries: \b \< \> ALL work on both bashes (GNU/glibc). Documented as GNU-glibc-specific, not POSIX-portable.
probe_results.S8_alternation: leftmost-LONGEST on both: (a|ab) AND (ab|a) on 'ab' -> 'ab'. DELTA vs PCRE leftmost-FIRST. Pinned divergence test in P1/P2.
probe_results.S9_locale_offsets: offsets/lengths are `${#...}` in the AMBIENT locale. ASCII exact. Multibyte: under empty/C locale on MSYS2 `${#}` BYTE-counts (2-byte é -> 2) while glibc regex CHAR-counts ('.' matches é as len 1) -> they DISAGREE; a full UTF-8 locale (C.UTF-8/en_US.UTF-8) makes both char-count (é -> 1). Documented; scan correctness locale-independent. nocasematch does NOT fold non-ASCII (É=~é -> no match) in this locale.
probe_results.S10_nonparticipating_groups: IDENTICAL on 5.2 and 5.3 (plan flagged as a risk — it is NOT). (a)|(b) on 'b' -> BASH_REMATCH=([0]=b [1]='' [2]=b), dense array, non-participating group = EMPTY STRING (indistinguishable from an empty match; documented). Copy to RESULT_GROUPS immediately.
probe_results.S11_replacement_grammar: design/DocWiki pin deferred to P3 (not a bash probe).
probe_results.S12_split_semantics: .NET pin deferred to P2 (not a bash probe).
probe_results.ADJ_bash_rematch_cleared_on_fail: a FAILED [[ =~ ]] CLEARS BASH_REMATCH (nelem 0) on BOTH bashes -> copy groups immediately after a successful match, before any other match.
probe_results.ADJ_no_multiline:
^ and $ anchor only at string start/end, never at 
 (^y on 'x
y' and x$ on 'x
y' both rc=1). Confirms roMultiLine wontfix.
probe_results.ADJ_quantified_group: (.)+ on 'abc' leaves the LAST iteration ('c') in group 1 (POSIX; matches PCRE).

execution_log.contract_pin: MEMBER TYPE = `static proc` for ALL members (was tentatively `static func` in the skeleton). Probed on both bashes: for a kklass STATIC class, `static func` ECHOES $RESULT on EVERY call (direct too, not just under $()) — pollutes stdout for rc-drivers like `if TRegEx.isMatch ...` and can carry only ONE of match's four return values. `static proc` is SILENT and sets the RESULT* globals. NB this is NOT the tstopwatch instance-`func` contract (which echoes only under $()) — static func != instance func on the echo axis. tregex contract: CALL DIRECTLY, read RESULT/RESULT_INDEX/RESULT_LENGTH/RESULT_GROUPS; NO $() capture (subshell loses the globals). OPEN for owner at P0 review: whether escape/replace/match should additionally body-echo their scalar for $() ergonomics (math-style) — default shipped is uniform-silent.
execution_log.skeleton: kcl/tregex/tregex.sh (re-source guard + kklass_pascal front-end + class TRegEx with 7 static-proc members + P0 stubs setting RESULT*/returning 0) + tests/tests.sh runner + tests/001_Skeleton.sh (7 cases: source+build, all-7-dispatch, direct-sets-RESULT, direct-silent, $()-no-leak, RESULT_*-shaped, PATH='' zero-fork). 7/7 on 5.2.37 AND true 5.3.9.
execution_log.baseline_remeasured: Master sweep on 5.2.37 GREEN: 15/15 suites, 0 failed (was 14 suites pre-tregex); 'All test suites passed.' tregex suite included at 7/7. Grand total 2528 tests = kcl 1764 (tcustomapplication 320, tdirectory 197, tfile 120, tlist 92, tpath 112, tstringhelper 393, tstringlist 155, dateutils 123, math 81, tdictionary 130, tstopwatch 34, tregex 7) + kklass 232 + kkore 232 + ktests 300. No regression possible-by-construction: tregex only ADDS a suite; no existing unit or kklass file was touched in P0.

execution_log.summary: Owner 'go P1' with both P0 decisions accepted (empty pattern -> rc2; escape/replace/replaceCb body-echo for $() ergonomics). Implemented the single-match core. Suite grew 7 -> 110 tests (001-005), 110/110 on 5.2.37 AND true 5.3.9.

implemented._match1: internal core (plain fn, dynamic-scope OUT params __tre_rc/__tre_m/__tre_g). Pattern via local __re; `[[ $1 =~ $__re ]] 2>/dev/null` (suppress 5.3 stderr, keep rc, fork-free). BASH_REMATCH copied IMMEDIATELY. i-flag: FORCE nocasematch on/off per the flag (deterministic — ambient shopt does NOT leak in), then restore caller's ambient state; no $().
implemented.isMatch: pure predicate: rc 0/1/2, writes NO RESULT* globals. Silent.
implemented.match: rc0 -> RESULT=matched, RESULT_INDEX=prefix-strip offset (S5 exact / S6 caveat), RESULT_LENGTH=${#matched} (ambient locale, S9), RESULT_GROUPS=BASH_REMATCH[1..] (non-participating='' S10). rc1/rc2 -> reset ''/-1/0/(). Silent (4 values, call direct).
implemented.escape: backslash-quotes ERE metaset \.^$*+?()[]{}| via a fork-free char loop (no sed); non-metas untouched; RESULT set AND body-echoes (owner P1) so `$(TRegEx.escape ...)` works.
implemented._invalid: debug-only (VERBOSE_KKLASS=debug) note on rc2; bash's own stderr always suppressed.

execution_log.tests: 001 wiring+contract (silent/no-$()-leak/escape-echo/pending-stubs/zero-fork); 002 isMatch (rc 0/1/2, anchors, POSIX classes, alternation, \b \< \>, i-flag, torture: newlines/globs/quotes/$(...)/backticks/unicode/empty/leading-dash, pattern-via-variable); 003 match (RESULT* tuple, S5 exact offset, S6 anchored-caveat pinned, empty match, S8 longest, S1 dot-newline, groups incl. non-participating S10, quantified-group last-iter ADJ3, no-match/invalid reset, BASH_REMATCH-copy safety, i-flag original-case); 004 escape (each metachar, no-meta untouched, all-metas, round-trip via isMatch/match, no-over-match, $()==RESULT, newline torture); 005 i-flag+fork-free (flag determinism vs ambient nocasematch, ambient restore on match/invalid paths, no-subshell proof, PATH='' on all 3 entry points, unrelated-shopt intact).
execution_log.test_fix: unicode length assertion in 003 was locale-dependent (S9: `${#é}`=2 bytes under empty/C locale on MSYS2, =1 char under UTF-8) — changed to assert text+offset (ASCII-robust) and RESULT_LENGTH==${#RESULT} (self-consistent) instead of hard-coding 1-vs-2.
execution_log.decisions_pending_owner: i-flag DETERMINISM: case sensitivity is decided SOLELY by the flag — a call WITHOUT 'i' FORCES case-sensitive matching even if the caller has `shopt -s nocasematch` globally (then restores it). Faithful to Delphi per-call roIgnoreCase and makes TRegEx deterministic regardless of shell state. Flagged for owner in case they prefer inheriting the ambient shopt.
execution_log.sweep_gate: Master sweep on 5.2.37 GREEN: 15/15 suites, 0 failed, 'All test suites passed.' Grand total 2631 tests = kcl 1867 (tcustomapplication 320, tdirectory 197, tfile 120, tlist 92, tpath 112, tstringhelper 393, tstringlist 155, dateutils 123, math 81, tdictionary 130, tstopwatch 34, tregex 110) + kklass 232 + kkore 232 + ktests 300. tregex 7 -> 110 (P0 skeleton -> P1 full). No regression: no existing unit or kklass file touched.

execution_log.summary: Owner 'go p2'. Implemented the global-scan pair. Design VALIDATED by a standalone prototype on both bashes before wiring: matches scan (remainder + empty-match advance-by-one) and split ASSEMBLED BY ABSOLUTE POSITION text[prevEnd:matchStart] — gives .NET Regex.Split parity incl. empty-match patterns, captured-group interleave, and kept leading/trailing/consecutive empties. Suite 110 -> 144 (006 matches, 007 split; 001 pending-stub list trimmed to replace/replaceCb). 144/144 on 5.2.37 AND true 5.3.9.

implemented.matches: matches text pattern outTexts [outOffsets|-] [flags]. Leftmost-longest scan on the remainder via _match1; RESULT=count; fills outTexts nameref (lossless) and optional outOffsets (absolute prefix-strip offsets). EMPTY match advances by ONE char (.NET parity: e.g. matches('abc','x*')=4 empty pieces). rc 0(>=1)/1(none)/2(invalid). Silent -> call direct ($() discards fills). Flags-without-offsets via '-' placeholder in arg 4.
implemented.split: split text pattern outArr [flags]. Emits pieces BETWEEN matches assembled by absolute position; captured groups (BASH_REMATCH[1..]) INTERLEAVED (S12); leading/trailing/consecutive empties KEPT; no-match -> whole text as one piece; RESULT=piece count; rc 0/2. Silent.
implemented.anchor_caveat: matches/split scan the REMAINDER, so ^ $ \b \< \> re-anchor per remainder (bash cannot \G-anchor). Unanchored patterns exact; anchored ones documented + PINNED by a test (matches('abc','^.')=[a,b,c]).

execution_log.reserved_names: output arrays must not be named __trx_texts / __trx_offs / __trx_out (nameref self-reference). Documented in tregex.sh headers.
execution_log.tests: 006 matches (count+array, offsets, empty-match .NET parity, greedy, no-match/invalid rc, i-flag via '-' placeholder, anchor caveat pin, $()-no-fill, torture: newlines/quotes/unicode/empty subject, zero-fork); 007 split (core .NET semantics, captured-group interleave, empties kept, no-match whole, empty-match char-split, multichar/regex/whitespace delimiters, i-flag, rc 0/2, $()-no-fill, round-trip join, torture: newline/unicode/quotes, zero-fork).
execution_log.harness_note: the standalone SMOKE harness (not the ktest suite) initially showed 3 false 'FAIL's — `((pass++))` returns rc1 when pass==0, and a couple of $?/expectation slips — all harness bugs, implementation was correct; the ktest suite (which counts correctly) is 144/144.
execution_log.sweep_gate: Master sweep on 5.2.37 GREEN: 15/15 suites, 0 failed, 'All test suites passed.' Grand total 2665 tests = kcl 1901 (tcustomapplication 320, tdirectory 197, tfile 120, tlist 92, tpath 112, tstringhelper 393, tstringlist 155, dateutils 123, math 81, tdictionary 130, tstopwatch 34, tregex 144) + kklass 232 + kkore 232 + ktests 300. tregex 110 -> 144 (P1 -> P2). No regression: no existing unit or kklass file touched. NB the first P2-gate attempt reported rc1 with empty output — the Bash tool cwd had drifted to kcl/tregex/tests so the RELATIVE 'tests/tests.sh' was not found (127); re-run with the ABSOLUTE master path /c/projects/kkbot/kbool/tests/tests.sh.

execution_log.batch: P3 + FPC cross-check integration + retro P1/P2
execution_log.summary: Owner 'go P3', then asked whether the FPC test corpus was used -> it was NOT and the plan's 'no FPC seed' premise was WRONG (unverified). Found FPC packages/vcl-compat: system.regularexpressions.pp = Delphi-compatible TRegEx over PCRE2, tests utcregexapi.pas (TRegEx API) + utcregex.pas (TPerlRegEx). Owner directed: fold FPC cross-check into P3, retro-check P1/P2, verify, then write a dialect report. All done. Suite 144 -> 190; 190/190 on 5.2.37 AND true 5.3.9.

implemented_P3.replace: replace text pattern replacement [maxCount|-] [flags]. Replace-ALL default; maxCount caps REPLACEMENTS. $-grammar via TRegEx._expandRepl: $$->$, $&/$0->whole, $1..$9->group(single digit), ${n}->group n(any width), out-of-range/unknown $x kept LITERAL, sed & backslash LITERAL (only $ special). Sets RESULT + BODY-ECHOES (owner P0). Invalid pattern -> text unchanged, rc2.
implemented_P3.replaceCb: replaceCb text pattern cbName [maxCount|-] [flags]. Callback invoked as cbName '<wholeMatch>' '<g1>' '<g2>' ... and MUST set REPLY to the replacement (fork-free; no echo). Shares TRegEx._replaceScan with replace (mode repl|cb).

fpc_integration.factual_fix: ledger test_basis + PLAN.md section 4 corrected: an FPC TRegEx seed EXISTS (over PCRE2, not a drop-in oracle). Was wrongly 'no FPC seed'.
fpc_integration.seed_file: tests/008_FpcParity.sh -- the dialect-compatible subset of utcregexapi.pas/utcregex.pas on FPC's fixture. Adjustments: FPC Match.Index 1-based -> ours 0-based (my=fpc-1); PCRE \s -> POSIX [[:space:]]. 15 FPC-traceable cases green -> confirms P1/P2/P3 match the FPC oracle where dialects agree.
fpc_integration.gap_closed: split gained maxCount/limit (Delphi Split(count) parity) -- FPC TestSplitLimit exposed the gap.
fpc_integration.dialect_report: docs/ERE-vs-PCRE.md -- full PCRE2-vs-POSIX-ERE difference catalogue grounded in the FPC tests; owner-requested for documentation.

execution_log.tests: 009_Replace.sh (26 invented: $-grammar, maxCount, i-flag, empty-match insert, anchor-caveat ^ per-remainder pinned, torture, invalid rc2, $() body-echo, replaceCb REPLY contract/groups/maxCount/empty-REPLY); 008 extended with 6 FPC replace/replaceCb cross-checks; 001 pending-stub test replaced (no stubs remain).
execution_log.test_fixes: two test-EXPECTATION slips (not impl bugs), fixed: (1) replace('abc','^','>') expected '>abc' but ^ re-anchors per remainder -> '>a>b>c>' (kept as a pinned anchor-caveat delta); (2) replaceCb '(.)(.)' on 'ab cd' -> '.' matches the space -> switched subject to 'abcd' -> 'badc'.

execution_log.bench: bash bench.sh on MSYS2, both bashes (5.2.37 / 5.3.9), all fork-free: raw [[ =~ ]] ~7us; TRegEx.isMatch ~80-90us (+ static-proc dispatch); match ~95-120us (+ offset+group copy); escape(43ch) ~0.4ms; matches ~115us/occurrence (linear, verified 10 vs 100); replace-all/split ~0.12-0.17ms/occurrence. Dispatch wrapper ~80us over the raw builtin. zero-fork check GREEN on every entry point. (Fixed a bench display bug: the per-occurrence summary was 10x inflated -- report line was per-call; corrected to tot/NS/count.)
